## Overall Highlights

- Direct commercial flights between the US and Venezuela have resumed after seven years, marking a significant development in bilateral relations.
- Passengers express relief and excitement about the renewed connectivity, feeling that Venezuela is no longer distant.
- The US government emphasizes strengthening ties with Venezuela through new agreements, while some European entities maintain calls for sanctions until democratic progress is observed.
- Venezuelan officials attribute economic challenges primarily to US sanctions, highlighting the ongoing debate over the impact of these measures.
- Both sides acknowledge the complexity of the relationship, with Venezuelan sources focusing on the positive aspects of renewed flights and US sources noting the limitations still faced by many Venezuelans.

<!--more-->

## Venezuelan Perspective (Venezuelan Sources)

- Commercial flights to the US have resumed after seven years, with passengers expressing excitement about the renewed connection.
- Venezuelan officials highlight the resumption of flights as a sign of improving relations with the US.
- The government emphasizes that sanctions are the main disruption to the economy, calling for their removal to facilitate further progress.
- Venezuela has signed agreements with US energy companies, indicating a potential thaw in economic relations.

## United States Perspective (United States Sources)

- The resumption of direct flights is seen as a cautious step towards improving US-Venezuela relations, but many Venezuelans still face barriers to travel.
- US officials note that while flights have resumed, significant issues remain, including the ongoing sanctions and lack of democratic reforms in Venezuela.
- The European Parliament has urged the continuation of sanctions against Venezuelan officials, reflecting a divide in international perspectives on the situation.
- Reports indicate that US officials were present on the first flight, highlighting the political significance of the event.
